siteConfig.podcastName: Empire Law Firm with Deborah Song

episode.title: The YOU Interview with Deborah Song
episode.description: Before you hire a personal injury lawyer in the Inland Empire, you should know who they really are. In this debut episode, Abby Arnold sits down with Deborah Song, founder of Inland Injury (dba of Empire Attorneys), for an unfiltered conversation about her path from middle-school political ambitions in the Inland Empire to becoming a Super Lawyers Rising Star and the only personal injury attorney operating full-time in Norco and Eastvale. Deborah shares her philosophy on client-first case strategy, why her firm pursues diminution-in-value and loss-of-use damages most firms skip, how she decides between negotiation and litigation, and why she's watching the Uber-backed California legislative push to cap contingency fees.

attorney.name: Deborah Song
attorney.title: Founder & Personal Injury Trial Attorney
bio: Deborah Song founded Empire Attorneys (doing business as Inland Injury) to bring Orange County-style personal injury representation back to the Inland Empire. She was born and raised in Moreno Valley, earned her B.A. from UC Riverside, and finished her J.D. at Western State University College of Law in Irvine while working full-time at Orange County personal injury trial firms. She is licensed by the State Bar of California (Bar No. 337172).

The firm handles personal injury matters across California — primarily car, truck, motorcycle, and pedestrian accidents, plus premises liability, wrongful death, and catastrophic injury cases — from offices in Eastvale and Norco. Deborah is, by her own count, the only full-time personal injury attorney practicing in Norco and Eastvale. She's a Super Lawyers Rising Star (peer-nominated), a member of the Hispanic Bar Association of the Inland Empire and the Mexican Bar Association, and Secretary of the Norco Rotary Club.

Deborah's firm is unusual in how aggressively it pursues property-damage-adjacent damages most personal injury firms ignore: diminution in value, loss of use, and GAP-insurance coordination. She has built a dedicated property-damage department because, in her words, 'making the client whole' has to include the market value the car loses the moment it's reported in a collision. Phillip B. Nghiem (Of Counsel, CA Bar #291525) joins her on select trial matters.

contact.address: Eastvale, California — serving the Inland Empire, Orange County, and statewide California

faqGroups.label: About the Podcast
faqGroups.description: Learn more about Empire Law Firm with Deborah Song and what you can expect from each episode.

questions.question: What topics does the Empire Attorneys podcast cover?
questions.answer: The podcast covers personal injury and car accident topics relevant to Inland Empire and California residents. Deborah Song discusses case strategy, negotiation versus litigation, California-specific insurance issues (particularly uninsured and underinsured motorist coverage), property damage damages most firms skip (loss of use, diminution in value, GAP coordination), and consumer protection / identity theft matters her firm also handles. Each episode is an unedited conversation, not a marketing reel.

questions.question: Who hosts the podcast?
questions.answer: Abby Arnold hosts the podcast and interviews Deborah Song, founder of Empire Attorneys (dba Inland Injury) in Eastvale, California. Deborah Song is admitted to the State Bar of California (Bar No. 337172) and has been recognized as a Super Lawyers Rising Star by peer nomination.

questions.question: How often are new episodes released?
questions.answer: New episodes are released on a regular cadence. Subscribe via the platform links in the footer to get notified when each episode drops.

questions.question: Can I submit questions or topic suggestions?
questions.answer: Yes. Contact Empire Attorneys through the firm's website at inlandinjury.com or call (951) 456-3891. The firm welcomes listener questions, particularly from Inland Empire residents with questions about their own auto policy, a recent collision, or identity-theft disputes with credit bureaus.

faqGroups.label: Legal Questions
faqGroups.description: Common California personal injury questions answered by Deborah Song of Empire Attorneys.

questions.question: What should I do immediately after a car accident in the Inland Empire?
questions.answer: Seek medical attention even if injuries seem minor, document the scene with photos, exchange information with other drivers, and file a police report. Do not give a recorded statement to any insurance adjuster — yours or the other driver's — before speaking with a California personal injury attorney. Deborah Song recommends contacting a lawyer early so the firm can open the claim with the carrier directly and send a letter of representation; any statement you make to your own insurer or the defendant's insurer can be twisted and used against you later.

questions.question: How long do I have to file a personal injury claim in California?
questions.answer: California Code of Civil Procedure Section 335.1 sets a two-year statute of limitations for most personal injury claims, running from the date of the accident or injury. Claims against government entities under the Government Claims Act (Gov. Code §911.2) require a written claim within six months. Missing the deadline typically forfeits the claim entirely, so early consultation matters. Empire Attorneys files suit before the two-year deadline in active cases even when the client is still treating, purely to protect the statute.

questions.question: What types of compensation can I recover in a California personal injury case?
questions.answer: Economic damages (medical bills, lost wages, future care, property damage), non-economic damages (pain and suffering, emotional distress, loss of consortium), and — in cases of malice, oppression, or fraud — punitive damages under California Civil Code Section 3294. California is a pure comparative fault state (Li v. Yellow Cab Co. 1975), so recovery is reduced by the plaintiff's percentage of fault rather than barred. Empire Attorneys also pursues diminution in value, loss of use, and GAP-insurance coordination — damages most personal injury firms leave on the table.

questions.question: Does my insurance premium go up if I file a claim after an accident that wasn't my fault?
questions.answer: Under California law, your insurer cannot raise your premium solely because you filed a claim. Premium increases require an at-fault finding on your driving record. Deborah Song stresses this because clients often avoid using their own uninsured/underinsured motorist (UM/UIM) coverage or medical-payments (MedPay) coverage for fear of a rate hike — leaving compensation on the table when the at-fault driver is uninsured, underinsured, or absent.

questions.question: What is diminution in value and why does Empire Attorneys pursue it?
questions.answer: Diminution in value is the market-value loss your vehicle suffers the moment it is reported in a collision — even after the physical damage is fully repaired. California common law allows a personal injury plaintiff to recover damages to make the plaintiff whole; a fixed bumper does not make the plaintiff whole if the car's resale or trade-in value has dropped. Most personal injury firms ignore this because the insurance adjuster refuses it. Empire Attorneys pursues it, including through litigation when necessary.

questions.question: Why does Deborah Song recommend UM/UIM coverage for every California driver?
questions.answer: California only requires 15/30/5 liability coverage (Vehicle Code §16056), and roughly 17% of California drivers carry no insurance at all. If the at-fault driver has no insurance or low limits and you have serious injuries, uninsured/underinsured motorist coverage under your own policy is often the only meaningful source of compensation. Deborah Song describes UM/UIM and MedPay as the real reason to carry insurance — not to protect your assets, but to protect yourself from the uninsured drivers she sees every week.

content.heroTitle: Empire Law Firm with Deborah Song
content.heroDescription: Join Deborah Song, founder of Inland Injury (dba Empire Attorneys), as she shares how her firm handles personal injury and car accident cases across the Inland Empire and California. Each episode is an unfiltered conversation about case strategy, California insurance coverage, and the property-damage issues most personal injury firms skip — all from the only full-time personal injury attorney operating in Norco and Eastvale.
content.articleTitle: Super Lawyers Rising Star Serving the Inland Empire and Statewide California
articleParagraphs: Empire Law Firm with Deborah Song brings the strategy and client-first philosophy Empire Attorneys uses on real cases directly to Inland Empire drivers. Deborah Song built the firm specifically to bring Orange County-style personal injury representation back to the region she grew up in — the only full-time personal injury attorney practicing in Norco and Eastvale. She is admitted to the State Bar of California (Bar No. 337172) and a peer-nominated Super Lawyers Rising Star.

Each episode addresses the concerns Inland Empire accident victims actually raise: when your own insurance is required to disclose policy limits (and when the other driver's is not), why uninsured/underinsured motorist coverage matters in a state where 17% of drivers carry none, what diminution in value is and why most firms never ask for it, how GAP coverage windows expire in 30–60 days, and how the firm decides whether a case settles, litigates, or goes to trial. Phillip B. Nghiem (Of Counsel, CA Bar #291525) — a Super Lawyers Rising Star 2022–2023 and part of the team behind a $52.9 million verdict in 2022 — joins on select trial matters.
content.featuredQuote: If you wait around, not great things are going to happen for you. The insurance company's goal is to see who blinks first — and the only way they take you seriously is if they know you'll actually try the case.

stats.casesVerbalization: Deborah Song has helped recover more than $50 million for injured Californians over the course of her career. Of Counsel Phillip B. Nghiem was part of a trial team that secured a $52.9 million verdict in 2022. Past results do not guarantee a similar outcome in any future case.

footer.description: Empire Law Firm with Deborah Song is a podcast hosted by Empire Attorneys (dba Inland Injury), a California personal injury firm founded by Deborah Song and operating from Eastvale and Norco. The firm handles personal injury, car accident, motorcycle accident, truck accident, premises liability, wrongful death, and catastrophic injury matters throughout California, and consumer identity-theft matters nationally.

about.title: About Deborah Song and Empire Attorneys
about.description: Empire Attorneys was founded by Deborah Song to bring Orange County-style personal injury representation back to the Inland Empire community she grew up in. The firm operates from Eastvale and Norco, represents clients across California, and takes identity-theft consumer protection cases nationally under federal law. Deborah is admitted to the State Bar of California (Bar No. 337172), a peer-nominated Super Lawyers Rising Star, and — by her own count — the only full-time personal injury attorney practicing in Norco and Eastvale.
highlights: $50M+ recovered for injured Californians (Deborah Song, career total)

podcastTeam.name: Deborah Song
podcastTeam.bio: Deborah Song founded Empire Attorneys to bring Orange County-style personal injury representation to the Inland Empire. Admitted to the State Bar of California (Bar No. 337172). Super Lawyers Rising Star. Hosts the podcast and leads the firm's Eastvale and Norco practice.

podcastTeam.name: Phillip B. Nghiem
podcastTeam.bio: Phillip B. Nghiem is Of Counsel to Empire Attorneys. Admitted to the State Bar of California (Bar No. 291525). Part of the trial team that secured a $52.9 million verdict in 2022. Super Lawyers Rising Star 2022–2023. Guest on select trial-strategy episodes.

links.title: Car Accident Lawyers
links.description: Empire Attorneys represents drivers and passengers injured in serious Inland Empire collisions — roughly 95% of the firm's caseload.

links.title: Truck Accident Lawyers
links.description: Commercial trucking cases involve federal motor carrier regulations and multiple corporate defendants — Deborah Song litigates them aggressively.

links.title: Motorcycle Accident Lawyers
links.description: Motorcycle cases involve insurer bias and California pure comparative fault rules. Empire Attorneys pushes back on assumptions about rider conduct.

links.title: Pedestrian Accident Lawyers
links.description: Pedestrian strikes often involve disputed fault and driver distraction. Empire Attorneys investigates from scene to courtroom.

links.title: Catastrophic Injuries
links.description: Catastrophic injury cases require life-care planning for future treatment, assistive equipment, and lost earning capacity — the firm builds damages models accordingly.

links.title: Wrongful Death
links.description: California Code of Civil Procedure Section 377.60 governs wrongful death claims. Empire Attorneys represents surviving spouses, children, and parents.

links.title: Premises Liability
links.description: Slip-and-fall, negligent security, and property-condition cases under California Civil Code Section 1714 — the firm handles these alongside motor vehicle matters.

links.title: Construction Injury
links.description: Third-party construction site injuries outside workers' compensation — the firm pursues them through civil litigation when employer-immunity rules do not apply.

links.title: Diminution in Value
links.description: The firm's signature damages claim. Deborah Song litigates diminution-in-value cases most personal injury firms refuse to pursue.

links.title: Loss of Use
links.description: When the at-fault carrier disputes liability and refuses to pay for a rental, loss of use damages accrue daily — the firm has filed suit purely on loss of use.

links.title: Total Loss and GAP Coordination
links.description: GAP insurance claims expire in 30–60 days. Empire Attorneys coordinates total-loss settlement and GAP filings to stop clients from being left upside-down on a loan.

links.title: Property Damage Negotiations
links.description: Rental coordination, inspection setup, and fair-market-value challenges — a dedicated in-firm department handles these alongside the bodily injury claim.

links.title: Uninsured/Underinsured Motorist Claims
links.description: Deborah Song discusses in Episode 1 why UM/UIM coverage matters — California requires only 15/30/5 and roughly 17% of drivers are uninsured.

links.title: Insurance Bad Faith
links.description: When carriers deny valid claims or delay payment, the firm pursues bad-faith remedies under California Insurance Code Section 790.03.

links.title: Identity Theft / Consumer Protection
links.description: Federal FCRA and California consumer protection claims against Experian, TransUnion, and Equifax — one of the firm's less-common practice areas.

links.title: Case Results
links.description: $50M+ recovered by Deborah Song over her career. Past results do not guarantee a similar outcome in your case.

chaptersDescription: In Episode 1, Deborah Song walks through her origin story — from watching C-SPAN in middle school to graduating Western State Law while working full-time at Orange County trial firms — why she came back to the Inland Empire to open Empire Attorneys, the property-damage damages (diminution in value, loss of use, GAP coordination) most personal injury firms skip, how she decides between negotiation and litigation, why UM/UIM coverage matters in California, and her take on AI and the Uber-backed push to cap contingency fees.

bio: Deborah Song founded Empire Attorneys (doing business as Inland Injury) after growing up in Moreno Valley and earning her B.A. from UC Riverside. She started law school at Whittier Law (which closed during her first year), transferred to Western State University College of Law in Irvine to keep her part-time night program, and worked daytime hours at Orange County personal injury trial firms throughout law school to build trial-ready experience before graduation.

Over her career, Deborah Song has helped recover more than $50 million for injured Californians. She is a peer-nominated Super Lawyers Rising Star and is, by her own count, the only full-time personal injury attorney practicing in Norco and Eastvale. She deliberately returned to the Inland Empire after Orange County practice to bring aggressive OC-style representation — especially on property-damage-adjacent damages like diminution in value, loss of use, and GAP coordination — to a region she says is consistently under-served.

Beyond her practice, Deborah Song is a member of the Hispanic Bar Association of the Inland Empire and the Mexican Bar Association (focused on community outreach and educating Inland Empire residents on their rights regardless of immigration status) and serves as Secretary of the Norco Rotary Club, which supports local veterans, senior communities, and student scholarships.
memberships: Hispanic Bar Association of the Inland Empire

bio: Phillip B. Nghiem is Of Counsel to Empire Attorneys and is admitted to the State Bar of California (Bar No. 291525). He was part of the trial team that secured a $52.9 million verdict in 2022 and has separately negotiated a $1.3 million settlement on behalf of injured Californians. Past results do not guarantee a similar outcome in any future case.

Phillip has been peer-nominated as a Super Lawyers Rising Star in 2022 and 2023 — recognition limited to approximately 2.5% of eligible California attorneys. He trained through the OCBA College of Trial Advocacy and Trial Lawyers University and joins Empire Attorneys on select trial matters, particularly complex insurance bad-faith and catastrophic-injury cases. disclaimers.text: This is an advertisement for legal services under California Rules of Professional Conduct 7.1–7.5 and California Business & Professions Code Sections 6157–6159.2. The information on this website and podcast is for general informational purposes only and does not constitute legal advice.

Viewing this site, listening to the podcast, or communicating with Empire Attorneys does not create an attorney-client relationship. No attorney-client relationship exists unless and until a written fee agreement has been signed by both the client and an Empire Attorneys lawyer.

Past results do not guarantee future outcomes. Each case is unique and must be evaluated on its own merits. The outcome of any prior case — including the $50M+ career recovery reference, the $52.9M 2022 verdict, and the $1.3M settlement — does not predict a similar result in your case.

Any client testimonials, endorsements, or reviews shown on this site reflect the individual experiences of those clients, are not a guarantee of similar results, and were not compensated (California RPC 7.1).

Empire Attorneys is licensed to practice in the State of California. This website is not intended as a solicitation for legal services in any jurisdiction where our attorneys are not licensed to practice. Identity theft and federal consumer protection matters may involve representation outside California under federal law.

Contingency Fee Notice: No attorney's fees are charged unless a recovery is obtained on your behalf. In California personal injury matters, the firm's pre-litigation contingency is 33 1/3% and post-filing contingency is 40%, consistent with industry norms discussed on the podcast. Clients may be responsible for case-related costs (expert witness fees, court costs, medical records) as outlined in a written retainer agreement.

Content shared on Empire Law Firm with Deborah Song is for general informational and educational purposes only, is not legal advice, and should not be relied upon as a substitute for consultation with a licensed California attorney about the specific facts of your matter.

The attorney responsible for the content of this podcast and website under California RPC 7.2 is Deborah Song (Cal. Bar No. 337172) of Empire Attorneys, 12495 Limonite Ave, Ste 1122, Eastvale, CA 92880. Phillip B. Nghiem (Cal. Bar No. 291525) serves as Of Counsel.

Super Lawyers Rising Star recognition (Deborah Song; Phillip B. Nghiem 2022–2023) is a peer-nomination rating limited to approximately 2.5% of eligible California attorneys, conferred by Thomson Reuters. Ratings and recognitions do not guarantee future results.
